mappings.rationale: Provides the overarching framework for understanding art across different cultures and periods, directly relevant to this section's survey of arts in particular cultures.
Covers the arts of ancient Greek and Roman civilizations, directly relevant to the section's treatment of arts of antiquity in the Western tradition.
Covers the civilization whose art is explicitly listed under arts of antiquity in the Western tradition.
Directly addresses the arts of ancient Egypt, explicitly named in the section's outline of arts in antiquity.
Covers the culture whose arts are explicitly listed under arts of antiquity — ancient Greek and Hellenistic arts.
Explores mythological subjects that were central to ancient Greek and Roman arts, relevant to the section's treatment of arts of antiquity.
Directly addresses literature of classical antiquity, a key art form in the section's coverage of arts of the Western tradition in antiquity.
Covers the Hellenistic period whose arts are explicitly named in the section's outline of Western antiquity.
Covers Roman civilization whose arts are explicitly listed under arts of antiquity in the Western tradition.
Relevant to the section's coverage of ancient Roman arts in the Western tradition.
Covers the transition from Roman to Early Christian arts, both explicitly named in the section's outline of arts in antiquity.
Etruscan art is a significant precursor to Roman art, relevant to the section's coverage of arts of antiquity in the Western tradition.
Directly relevant to the section's explicit listing of arts of the ancient Middle East in the Western antiquity outline.
Assyrian art is a major tradition within the ancient Middle East arts explicitly mentioned in the section.
Babylonian art is part of the ancient Middle East artistic tradition explicitly mentioned in the section.
Egyptian hieroglyphic art is a distinctive art form of ancient Egypt, relevant to the section's coverage of arts of antiquity.
Directly relevant to the section's coverage of arts of the Middle Ages in the Western tradition.
Covers literature of the Middle Ages, a key art form in the section's treatment of medieval Western arts.
Directly relevant to the section's coverage of arts from the Renaissance to the present in the Western tradition.
Specifically covers Renaissance visual arts, directly named in the section's outline of Western arts from the Renaissance onward.
Covers Victorian-era culture including arts, relevant to the section's treatment of Western arts from the Renaissance to the present.
Covers a major artistic movement spanning literature, music, and visual arts in the Western tradition from the 18th-19th centuries.
Covers the modernist movement across multiple art forms in the Western tradition, relevant to arts from the Renaissance to the present.
Directly covers modern visual arts in the Western tradition, part of the section's scope from the Renaissance to the present.
Covers the most recent developments in Western art, completing the section's timeline from the Renaissance to the present.
Covers major 20th-century Western art movements, relevant to the section's treatment of arts from the Renaissance to the present in Europe.
Covers avant-garde movements in 20th-century Western art, relevant to arts from the Renaissance to the present.
Covers the Futurist movement in European art and literature, relevant to the section's treatment of Western arts from the Renaissance to the present.
Covers the Decadent movement in late 19th-century European art and literature, relevant to arts in the Western tradition.
Explores Bohemian artistic culture in Europe, relevant to the section's treatment of Western arts from the Renaissance to the present.
Covers a major American arts movement, relevant to the section's treatment of arts from the Renaissance to the present in America.
Covers the development of American arts and culture, relevant to the section's treatment of Western arts in America.
Relevant to the section's coverage of arts of East Asian peoples, specifically China.
Relevant to the section's coverage of arts of East Asian peoples, specifically Japan.
Relevant to the section's explicit listing of Korean arts under East Asian arts.
Directly covers a major art form of China, explicitly listed under East Asian arts in the section.
Directly covers a major art form of Japan, explicitly listed under East Asian arts in the section.
Confucian aesthetics profoundly shaped East Asian arts in China, Japan, and Korea — cultures explicitly listed in the section.
Relevant to the section's coverage of arts of Tibet and Himalayan countries under Central Asian arts.
Hindu religious traditions are deeply intertwined with South Asian arts in India and Sri Lanka, explicitly listed in the section.
Directly covers a major art form of India, relevant to the section's treatment of South Asian arts.
Relevant to the section's coverage of South Asian arts, specifically India.
Directly relevant to the section's explicit listing of arts of Southeast Asian peoples including Burma, Cambodia, Indonesia, and others.
Islamic traditions profoundly shaped the arts of the Middle East and Islamic peoples, a major section of this outline.
Provides historical context for the arts of the Middle East and Islamic peoples, a dedicated subsection of this outline.
Covers Islamic cultural traditions across regions, relevant to the section's treatment of arts of the Islamic peoples.
Relevant to the section's explicit coverage of arts of the Jewish peoples under Middle Eastern arts.
Provides historical context for the arts of the Jewish peoples, explicitly listed in the section.
Directly covers literature as an art form of the Jewish peoples, explicitly listed in the section.
Provides historical context for the arts of African peoples, covering Sudanic, Central, East, Southern, and West African cultures listed in the section.
African religious traditions are deeply connected to African arts, relevant to the section's coverage of arts of African peoples.
Covers Aztec civilization whose arts fall under the section's treatment of arts of Meso-American peoples.
Covers Maya civilization whose arts fall under the section's treatment of arts of Meso-American peoples.
Directly relevant to the section's explicit coverage of arts of the North American Indian peoples.
Covers literature as an art form of North American Indian peoples, explicitly listed in the section.
Relevant to the section's coverage of arts of Meso-American and South American peoples, blending indigenous and colonial traditions.
Celtic art traditions are relevant to the section's coverage of Western arts in antiquity and the Middle Ages.
Viking art and material culture are relevant to the section's coverage of Western arts in the Middle Ages.
Relevant to the section's coverage of Central Asian arts, including the arts of nomadic peoples.
The Silk Road facilitated artistic exchange between East Asian, Central Asian, South Asian, and Middle Eastern cultures — all covered in this section.
Byzantine art bridges ancient Roman and medieval Western arts, relevant to the section's treatment of Early Christian arts and arts of the Middle Ages.
Directly relevant to the section's coverage of arts of Stone Age peoples.
The Crusades produced significant cross-cultural artistic exchange between Western and Middle Eastern traditions, relevant to arts of the Middle Ages.
Archaeological methods are essential for studying arts of Stone Age peoples and ancient civilizations covered throughout this section.
Anthropological approaches are essential for understanding arts of non-Western peoples including African, Oceanian, and American Indian cultures covered in this section.
Directly relevant to the section's coverage of Early Christian arts and medieval Western arts.
Covers samurai culture and its distinctive artistic traditions in Japan, relevant to the section's treatment of East Asian arts.
Egyptian mythology shaped ancient Egyptian arts, relevant to the section's coverage of arts of antiquity.
Druidic culture influenced Celtic arts, relevant to the section's coverage of Western arts in antiquity.
Relevant to the section's coverage of European arts from the Middle Ages through the Renaissance and beyond.
Habsburg patronage shaped European arts from the Renaissance onward, relevant to the section's treatment of Western arts.
Pagan religious traditions shaped arts of pre-Christian and non-Western cultures covered in this section.
Relevant to the section's explicit coverage of arts of the Australian Aboriginal peoples under Oceanian arts.
Relevant to the section's explicit listing of Pakistan under South Asian arts.
Iranian art is a major tradition within Middle Eastern and Islamic arts, both explicitly covered in this section.
The Trojan War is a major subject of ancient Greek arts, relevant to the section's coverage of ancient Greek and Hellenistic arts.
Spartan culture and its artistic expressions are part of ancient Greek arts covered in the section.
Folk music traditions are relevant to the section's cross-cultural survey of arts, particularly primitive and folk arts across cultures.
Provides methodology for studying music across the diverse cultures covered in this section.
Covers musical traditions across global cultures, directly relevant to this section's survey of arts in particular cultures worldwide.
The Conquest significantly affected Meso-American and South American arts, relevant to the section's coverage of arts of American Indian peoples.
Indian philosophical traditions deeply influenced South Asian artistic expression, relevant to the section's coverage of arts of South Asian peoples.
Rastafari culture produced distinctive arts blending African and Caribbean traditions, relevant to the section's coverage of arts of African peoples and diaspora.
The Beat movement is a significant American arts movement, relevant to the section's coverage of arts from the Renaissance to the present in America.
Alexander's conquests spread Hellenistic arts across a vast area, relevant to the section's coverage of Hellenistic arts and arts of Central and South Asia.
French cinema is a major European art form, relevant to the section's coverage of Western arts from the Renaissance to the present.
British cinema is a significant European art form, relevant to the section's coverage of Western arts from the Renaissance to the present.
Garden design is a significant art form across cultures, relevant to the section's cross-cultural survey of artistic traditions.
Modern architecture is relevant to the section's coverage of Western arts from the Renaissance to the present.
British architecture is relevant to the section's coverage of Western arts in the European tradition.
Decolonization shaped arts of African, Asian, and Oceanian peoples, relevant to understanding the cultural contexts of non-Western arts covered in this section.
Gender perspectives on arts across cultures are relevant to understanding the social context of artistic production throughout the cultures covered in this section.
